key_uncertainties: structure of integration with loyalty program
size of gap versus land-based competitors' co-branded programs
2025-Q2.mgmt_statement_summary: Confirmed existence of a co-branded credit card and signaled a meaningful announcement was imminent in close partnership with the card provider. Card was not yet tied into the loyalty program at this point.

status_delta.detail: first quarter this asset appears in the lifecycle record

key_uncertainties: pace of cardholder growth toward another doubling
repeat-guest booking-window and spend lift attributable to the card
share of loyalty-tier upgrades driven by Status Match plus the card
2026-Q1.mgmt_statement_summary: Launched Royal ONE co-branded credit card as part of an expanded loyalty ecosystem alongside Points Choice and Status Match. Positioned as the most powerful way for guests to earn and redeem points across brands. Cardholder accounts have more than doubled since 2019, with management citing an opportunity to double again as integration deepens.

asked_by_history.framing: Co-branded credit card and loyalty program as the key gap versus land-based competitors, including the fact that the card was not yet tied to the loyalty program
Implications of the new co-branded card and loyalty program changes for net yield growth, repeat-guest booking-window, and onboard spend

primary_framings_observed: Closing the loyalty and co-branded credit card gap versus land-based competitors
Integration of the card with the broader loyalty program and rewards ecosystem
Implications for net yield growth from higher repeat-guest penetration
Booking-window lengthening and onboard spend uplift from cardholders
Scaling of cardholder accounts and runway for further doubling

currently_open_questions_as_of_2026Q1: Quantified contribution of Royal ONE to 2026-2027 net yield growth
Cardholder account count today and trajectory toward management's implied second doubling
Differential booking-window, repeat rate, and onboard spend for Royal ONE cardholders versus non-cardholders
Economics of the co-brand agreement, including fee share and marketing support from the card provider
Interaction effects with Points Choice and Status Match on loyalty-tier migration
Penetration ceiling of the card across the Royal Caribbean, Celebrity, and Silversea guest bases
